stories.title: Quote of the day by Michael Schumacher: 'You win a race, the next race it’s a question mark. Are you still the best or not?'
stories.summary: Michael Schumacher emphasizes a vital truth: past victories are no assurance of future triumphs. In every event, champions must earn their recognition anew. This notion transcends sports, resonating within business and scientific realms alike. Ongoing dedication and adaptability are crucial for maintaining excellence and significance. True mastery is an evolving journey rather than a static achievement.

stories.title: Quote of the day by Fyodor Dostoevsky: 'What is hell? I maintain that it is the suffering of being unable to love'
stories.summary: Fyodor Dostoevsky posited that the inability to love results in deep suffering. His literary works, infused with his personal struggles and time in prison, delve into this theme. The notion of hell as an internal fracture feels particularly relevant in today's world, where virtual interactions often lack true empathy and connection. Dostoevsky's wisdom underscores the vital importance of authentic human relationships.

stories.title: Who is Connor Murphy’s alleged girlfriend? What we know about the late influencer’s relationship with the woman 10 years younger

After his death in Thailand, she said she had never seen him use drugs and could not explain his reported behaviour before the incident. Authorities are still investigating the case.

stories.title: Scientists invented a fake eye disease to see if AI chatbots could spot it, but the experiment took an unexpected turn
stories.summary: In a groundbreaking experiment, researchers created a fictitious eye disease, bixonimania, to evaluate the accuracy of AI chatbots. Many large language models accepted this non-existent condition as real, emphasizing the dangers of misinformation. Additionally, scientists found themselves referencing bogus research papers without realizing their inauthenticity, showcasing how AI learns patterns from the internet rather than confirming facts.

stories.title: Computer scientist who created world's first chatbot in the 1960s spent his whole life warning that AI should never replace humans, and the reason will shock you
stories.summary: In the 1960s, Joseph Weizenbaum pioneered the first chatbot, named Eliza, which unveiled a startling truth: humans could emotionally bond with machines. This insight led him to caution against the potential of AI usurping human empathy. Weizenbaum's assertions about the risks of deceiving machines fostering misplaced trust echo powerfully in today's landscape of artificial intelligence.

stories.title: One of the world's largest T. rex skeletons is heading to auction with a price tag of up to $30 million
stories.summary: A remarkable Tyrannosaurus rex fossil, affectionately dubbed Gus, is set to hit the auction block at Sotheby's. Measuring an impressive 38 feet in length, this nearly complete skeleton is anticipated to attract bids ranging from twenty to thirty million dollars. Its outstanding preservation and size underscore Gus's significance within the growing market for collectible dinosaur fossils.

Analysts say further fuel price hikes may be needed if crude stays elevated.

This surge occurred as oil prices climbed, fueling inflation worries among investors. Shorter-dated bond yields also increased, reflecting broader market pressures. Investors are watching a significant government bond auction scheduled for later today. Concerns over fiscal policy and potential inflation are driving these market movements.

stories.summary: British farmers are on the verge of integrating innovative plant wearables to enhance crop monitoring. Powered by moisture evaporation, the devices continuously operate, distinguishing between water and salinity stress while tracking growth alterations. A network of these wearables could yield unparalleled insight into field conditions from a plant's perspective.

stories.title: Iran's Khamenei buried in Mashhad four months after US-Israeli strikes killed him
stories.summary: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was buried in Mashhad after a week of ceremonies. He ruled Iran for nearly thirty-seven years and died in recent airstrikes. Large crowds gathered for funeral processions which brought daily life to a standstill. His eldest son led prayers, but his successor was notably absent from the burial. This burial occurred amid renewed hostilities and recent strikes between the US and Iran.

stories.title: Candace Owens under fire from conservatives; Tyler Robinson-Charlie Kirk case comments trigger fresh controversy
stories.summary: Candace Owens is facing criticism from several conservative commentators after questioning the case against Tyler Robinson, who is accused of killing Charlie Kirk. The dispute intensified as prosecutors introduced testimony, surveillance footage and other evidence during Robinson’s preliminary hearing.

stories.title: Andrew Tate links Connor Murphy’s death to Ayahuasca, claims psychedelic left influencer ‘insane’ before tragedy
stories.summary: Andrew Tate has linked Connor Murphy’s death to the influencer’s past use of Ayahuasca, claiming the psychedelic substance affected his mental health. His comments sparked debate online, with some users agreeing and others criticizing his remarks. However, authorities have not confirmed any connection between Murphy’s death and drug use. Autopsy and toxicology results are still awaited.

stories.title: Quote of the day by Franz Kafka: 'There are some things one can achieve only by a deliberate leap in the opposite direction'
stories.summary: True progress sometimes emerges from taking steps that seem counterproductive at first glance. Kafka's philosophy hints that, occasionally, achieving certain aspirations requires not only perseverance but also a deliberate detour. This notion extends into areas like psychology and artistic pursuits. In our rapidly evolving modern world, the strategy of intentional breaks becomes increasingly significant. Unlike aimlessly abandoning a direction, a well-considered pivot can yield transformative results.

Quote of the day by Diogenes: 'I threw away my cup when I saw a child drinking water from his hands'
stories.summary: Diogenes, the radical philosopher, famously threw away his cup when he witnessed a child quenching thirst using only their hands. This timeless tale encourages us to challenge our reliance on luxuries and conveniences. Cynic philosophy suggests that true freedom is found in having fewer possessions. In today's complex world, embracing simplicity can enhance our well-being, reminding us that valuable insights often arise from keen observation rather than formal learning.
